[redis/redis #14218] [BUG] HINCRBYFLOAT on HFE field removes field expiration on replica

When executing HINCRBYFLOAT on a field of HFE, the expiration time of that field is removed on the replica, even though the master retains it. This leads to an inconsistency between master and replica.

**To reproduce**

- master
[snippet]
127.0.0.1:6379> hset hash f1 1
(integer) 1
127.0.0.1:6379> hexpire hash 200 fields 1 f1
1) (integer) 1
127.0.0.1:6379> hincrbyfloat hash f1 1
"2"
127.0.0.1:6379> HTTL hash fields 1 f1
1) (integer) 187
[snippet]
127.0.0.1:6380> httl hash fields 1 f1  <== afte

Source: https://github.com/redis/redis/issues/14218

[redis/redis #2337] redis-benchmark does not report error when no auth is provided

Running redis-benchmark does not report error when test is running without the -a flag for a server which requires authentication. 

The results reported are effectively for time to get a (error) NOAUTH Authentication required. response rather than actually run the command. So, the benchmark reports incorrect results.

The fix could be to run a test ping to test whether authentication is required or not.

[nats-io/nats-server #432] Authorization Timeout and TLS

When TLS and authorization is enabled, the authorization timeout can fire during the TLS handshake, causing the server to write the authorization timeout error string into the client socket, injecting what becomes bad data into the TLS handshake.  This creates misleading errors on the client such as tls: oversized record received with length 21024.

I've only seen this happen when the host machine is under very heavy load with many clients simultaneously connecting, causing the server to become CPU bound.

[nats-io/nats-server #5] gnatsd HTTP monitoring has some issues

First, gnatsd takes a parameter to use as the HTTP monitoring port, however it doesn't actually use the passed parameter.  The port is hardcoded to 6062.

Second, gnatsd always starts the HTTP server, but only registers the handlers for the server if the monitoring port is specified.  The goroutine to launch the HTTP monitoring should be moved within the if statement to see if a monitoring port was specified.

[vitessio/vitess #19261] Bug Report: `EmergencyReparentShard` fails in GTID shard with any file-based replicas

### Overview of the Issue

If a shard with GTID-based tablets also contains one or more file-based tablets, EmergencyReparentShard will fail with: "encountered mix of GTID-based and non GTID-based relay logs", leaving unplanned failures of the PRIMARY un-actioned. This can lead to an indefinite outage a human _(or clever external automation)_ must manually respond to 👎 

Desired outcome: if we are certain the file-based replica is NOT a semi-sync acker _(shouldn't be, but theoretically possible)_, we should prioritize promoting a GTID-based replica vs. fail outright.

[vitessio/vitess #20362] Bug Report: VTOrc keeps stale `database_instance.hostname` on probe failure

### Overview of the Issue

When a tablet's MysqlHostname changes in topo _(eg: pod rescheduled to a new IP)_ AND the MySQL probe at the new address fails, VTOrc never updates database_instance.hostname — the row keeps pointing at the **previous** address indefinitely. The analyser then flags the tablet as InvalidReplica _(not actionable)_, so VTOrc neither corrects the row nor recovers it

This happens because ReadTopologyInstanceBufferable _(go/vt/vtorc/inst/instance_dao.go:385-405)_ only calls WriteInstance _(full REPLACE INTO)_ on probe success.

### Overview of the Issue

StreamExecute (the OLAP / streaming query path) bypasses the stored-procedure safety checks that the buffered Execute path enforces. As a result, two classes of CALL that Execute rejects are silently accepted when run through StreamExecute:

1. **Multi-resultset procedures return truncated results with no error.** A procedure that produces more than one resultset is rejected by Execute with Multi-Resultset not supported in stored procedure. Through StreamExecute it returns **only the first resultset and err == nil** — the caller silently gets partial/wrong data.

[vitessio/vitess #20365] Bug Report: field-metadata (impossible) queries drop SET_VAR hints, causing spurious only_full_group_by (1055) and other session-setting mismatches

### Overview of the Issue

For each Route, vtgate builds two SQL strings: the main Query, and a *field query* (an "impossible query" of the form select ... where 1 != 1) used to fetch column metadata from a tablet without returning rows.

The main query is serialized with the statement's comments, which include the /*+ SET_VAR(...) */ optimizer hints vtgate uses to propagate session system variables — most importantly sql_mode — down to MySQL. The field query, produced by FormatImpossibleQuery (go/vt/sqlparser/impossible_query.go), **drops those comments entirely**.
